title: Tokenization Could Be the Next ETF-Style Market Revolution
summary: Tokenization is following a similar path to ETFs, which grew into a $10 trillion market. This could revolutionize how assets are traded and owned, making complex assets more accessible and liquid.
body:
Tokenization, the process of converting real-world assets into digital tokens on a blockchain, is gaining traction in a way that mirrors the early days of exchange-traded funds (ETFs). Just as ETFs transformed traditional investing, tokenization could do the same for a wide range of assets, from real estate to fine art.

According to a commentary by Lie on CoinDesk, the current dialogue and patterns around tokenization resemble those of ETFs in their infancy. ETFs ultimately transformed into a $10+ trillion market, and tokenization could follow a similar trajectory as an ETF-style market structure revolution. This process makes traditionally illiquid assets more accessible and easier to trade, potentially democratizing investment opportunities.

The key to this revolution is the underlying blockchain technology, which tokenizes real-world assets to transform market structure. As more institutions and regulators embrace tokenization, we could see a significant shift in how assets are managed and traded globally, similar to how ETFs evolved over time.
twitterThread: Tokenization is following the ETF playbook, described as an 'ETF-style market structure revolution' that could unlock a $10+ trillion market. This mirrors how ETFs transformed investing.
Just as ETFs changed how people invest, tokenization could reshape how real-world assets like real estate and art are traded and owned, making them more accessible and liquid.

The key is blockchain technology tokenizing assets to transform market structure. Watch for institutional adoption as this trend evolves. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-04-tokenization-could-be-the-next-etf-style-market-revolution
standaloneTweet: Tokenization is following an ETF-style market structure revolution path, potentially unlocking a $10+ trillion market by making complex assets like real estate and art more accessible and liquid. This mirrors how ETFs democratized investing.
